report_name: Ransomware Evolves in 2026: Attackers Adopt Post-Quantum Crypto and Encryptionless Extortion

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A threat group pioneering the 'encryptionless extortion' model, where they gain access, exfiltrate sensitive data, and then threaten to leak it publicly, bypassing the data encryption step.

threat_actors.name: Initial Access Brokers
threat_actors.description: A class of threat actor that specializes in gaining access to corporate networks and selling that access to other malicious actors, such as ransomware groups. They commonly use compromised RDP, VPNs, and RDWeb as entry points.

threat_actors.name: Advanced Ransomware Groups
threat_actors.description: A general classification for sophisticated ransomware operators in 2026 who have adopted advanced tactics such as post-quantum cryptography, encryptionless extortion, and systematic EDR evasion using BYOVD techniques.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: A core ransomware tactic, now being augmented with post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms to ensure encrypted data remains secure against future quantum computing capabilities, increasing long-term extortion leverage.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: A core phase of modern ransomware attacks where attackers use 'EDR killers' and Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) techniques to terminate endpoint security software like MsMpEng.exe and SentinelAgent.exe before deploying their main payload.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: Leveraged in Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attacks to exploit a vulnerability in a legitimate, signed driver to execute malicious code with kernel-level permissions.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Used by Initial Access Brokers (IABs) to gain initial access to corporate networks, with compromised Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP), VPNs, and RDWeb being the most common entry points.

attack_patterns.name: Archive Collected Data
attack_patterns.description: Used in encryptionless extortion attacks to stage large amounts of sensitive data into archive files (.zip, .rar, .7z) on servers or workstations before exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: A method used in encryptionless extortion attacks to transfer large archives of stolen data to attacker-controlled cloud storage destinations.

attack_patterns.name: Kernel Modules and Extensions
attack_patterns.description: As part of the Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) technique, attackers load a known-vulnerable but legitimately signed driver into the kernel to subsequently exploit it for privilege escalation and defense evasion.

malware.name: EDR Killer
malware.description: A category of malicious tools specifically designed to terminate or disable Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agents and other security software as a precursor to a ransomware attack.

tools.name: Vulnerable Driver
tools.description: A legitimate, signed hardware driver with a known vulnerability, used in Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attacks. The attacker loads the driver to exploit it and gain kernel-level execution privileges.

vulnerabilities.name: Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A class of vulnerabilities in legitimate, signed hardware drivers that can be exploited by an attacker who has already gained administrative privileges. The exploit allows the execution of malicious code with kernel-level permissions, used to bypass security tools.

infrastructure.name: Exfiltration Cloud Storage
infrastructure.description: Cloud storage infrastructure used as a destination for large volumes of stolen data in encryptionless extortion attacks.

indicators.name: Suspicious Driver Load Event
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Driver Load Event
id: 2b9a1c3d-f4e5-4b6c-8d7e-9f0a1b2c3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects the installation of a new service, which can indicate a new driver being loaded. This is a potential sign of a Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attack.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/reviewing-the-trends-in-ransomware-attacks-in-2026/112674/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1547.006
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software installation or updates.
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: system
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 7045
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects Windows Event ID 7045, indicating a new service (and potentially a driver) was installed, which is a behavior seen in BYOVD attacks.

indicators.name: Potential Data Staging with Archiving Tool
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Staging with Archiving Tool
id: 8e7d6c5b-a4b3-4c2d-1b0a-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common archiving tools, which may be used by attackers to stage data for exfiltration, a common tactic in encryptionless extortion.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/reviewing-the-trends-in-ransomware-attacks-in-2026/112674/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: low
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of archiving tools by users or administrators.
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
      - '\winrar.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common archiving utilities, which could indicate data staging for exfiltration as seen in encryptionless extortion attacks.

indicators.name: Security Software Process Termination
indicators.pattern:
title: Security Software Process Termination
id: 5a4b3c2d-1e0f-9a8b-7c6d-5e4f3a2b1c0d
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to terminate common EDR and antivirus processes. This is a common precursor to ransomware deployment, often performed using 'EDR killers' or BYOVD techniques.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/reviewing-the-trends-in-ransomware-attacks-in-2026/112674/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_command:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\taskkill.exe'
      - '\pskill.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' /f '
  selection_process:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MsMpEng.exe'
      - 'SentinelAgent.exe'
      - 'SAVService.exe'
      - 'CSFalconService.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects the forceful termination of common security agent processes, a key step in EDR evasion.

relationships.target_name: Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver Vulnerability
Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver Vulnerability

relationships.source_name: Potential Data Staging with Archiving Tool
